title: float
text: float Uniface data type that holds a number with a floating decimal point. float ParameterName : Direction float VariableName Use Use in params and variables blocks to define the data type of a parameter or variable. The data type can be selected when defining fields, global variables, and component variables. Description Uniface internally supports a platform-independent precision of 38 digits for the mantissa, plus one for rounding, and four digits for the exponent, as follows: +/- (99,999,999,999,999,999,999,999,999,999,999,999,999e+/- 9999) When storing data, it depends on the DBMS whether this high precision is supported. Fields defined as data type float or numeric can accept a scientific notation. For example, you could enter 1.23e-5 in a field with data type float and 123e-5 in a field with data type numeric . If no display format is specified for a field with data type float, the field syntax length is used when formatting the value. Related Links Extracting Values From Numeric Data When you are working with data stored as a Numeric or Float value, you can extract information such as the integer part or the fractional part of a value. This extraction is done with a numeric extraction parameter. Packing Codes for Numeric Data The Uniface Numeric and Float data types are associated with many packing codes. Floating Point Packing Codes (F) When high precision is required, use the F packing code, which stores numbers as an exponent (2 n) and a mantissa (the number by which to multiply the exponent).
